<!-- AUTOGENERATED by praxis/docs.py - do not edit by hand -->
# Decoder block layouts

Top-level layer types the decoder stacks. Mix attention-based and recurrent designs freely.

Registry: ``praxis.BLOCK_REGISTRY`` (8 entries)

## `conv` - ConvolutionalBlock

A special kind of block that omits the self-attention mechanism, in favor of causal
convolutional layers and periodic activations.

Source: [praxis/blocks/conv.py:16](../praxis/blocks/conv.py#L16)

## `gru` - GRUBlock

A GRU block with SMEAR routing for multi-expert processing. Cleaner implementation
without complex masking.

Source: [praxis/blocks/gru.py:13](../praxis/blocks/gru.py#L13)

## `min` - MinGRUBlock

A minimally-recurrent network.

Source: [praxis/blocks/min.py:15](../praxis/blocks/min.py#L15)

## `mru` - MRUBlock

A recurrent model with efficient parallel scan. Based upon Matrix Recurrent Units:
https://github.com/mikayahlevi/mru-lm/tree/main

Source: [praxis/blocks/mru.py:15](../praxis/blocks/mru.py#L15)

## `nano` - NanoBlock

A special kind of block that omits all self-attention mechanisms, in favor of dense
layers with periodic activations. Inspired by NanoFFT:
https://github.com/timurgepard/nanoFFT I later found this same idea published in MLP-
Mixer: https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.01601

Source: [praxis/blocks/nano.py:14](../praxis/blocks/nano.py#L14)

## `recurrent` - RecurrentBlock

A recurrent block type.

Source: [praxis/blocks/recurrent.py:13](../praxis/blocks/recurrent.py#L13)

## `ssm` - SSMBlock

A simplified State Space Model (SSM) block for causal language modeling.

Source: [praxis/blocks/ssm.py:14](../praxis/blocks/ssm.py#L14)

## `transformer` - TransformerBlock

A standard transformer block, with adjustable feedforward "experts".

Source: [praxis/blocks/transformer.py:34](../praxis/blocks/transformer.py#L34)
